label: 336_payrolls_household survey_employment gains_job gains
description: The payroll employment report tracks job gains through the household and employer surveys. It shows fluctuations in monthly payrolls, highlighting periods of strong growth and slowdowns, providing insights into the overall health of the job market.

earliest_articles.title: Report on the December 2009 Job Situation
earliest_articles.excerpt: The latest employment report shows that the nation’s job market continues to make a painfully slow recovery from the worst labor market downturn since the Great Depression. The unemployment rate remained unchanged, though only because the number of Americans withdrawing from the labor force was somewhat larger than…

earliest_articles.title: The Jobs Report for January 2010
earliest_articles.excerpt: The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) employment report for January contains mixed signals about the job market’s emergence from the 2008-2009 recession. The household survey showed a sizeable drop in the unemployment rate – down 0.3 percentage points from December 2009 and down 0.4 percentage points since the peak…

earliest_articles.title: Note on the February 2010 Employment Situation
earliest_articles.excerpt: Even though the recovery of the job market remains painfully slow, the latest Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) employment report showed that the labor market continued to score gains in February. For the 25th time in the last 26 months, payroll employment declined in February, but the decline was very small. Payroll…

newest_articles.title: Job gains continued in 2016, but slow rebound in participation rate will limit future gains
newest_articles.excerpt: This post originally appeared on Real Clear Markets on December 21, 2016. Employers in the U.S. boosted payrolls again in 2016, giving the nation its seventh consecutive year of solid job gains. For the fifth year in a row employment gains topped 175,000 a month. In the 12 months ending in November, public and private…

newest_articles.title: Robust job gains and a continued rebound in  labor force participation
newest_articles.excerpt: The latest BLS jobs report shows little sign employers are worried about the future strength of the recovery. Both the employer and household surveys suggest U.S. employers have an undiminished appetite for new hires. Nonfarm payrolls surged 242,000 in February, and upward revisions BLS employment estimates for…

central_articles.title: Jobless Rate Stuck, But Employment Gains Remain Robust
central_articles.excerpt: The payroll employment report brought more good news in June as private employers added 202,000 jobs. A small decline in government payrolls meant that total payrolls rose 195,000. Upward revisions in job totals for April and May added 70,000 to the size of payrolls in May. Since the beginning of the year employers…

central_articles.title: Continued Weakness in Payroll Gains, but Good News on Unemployment
central_articles.excerpt: For the second month in a row the BLS employer survey showed slowing job gains compared with the average gains in the previous 17 months. Between July 2012 and November 2013, monthly payroll increases averaged 198,000 and never dipped below 141,000. Payrolls rose only 113,000 in January following an estimated gain of…

central_articles.title: Impressive Job Gains Cap a Year of Steady Labor Market Improvement
central_articles.excerpt: Employers added 252,000 to their payrolls in December capping the best year for employment gains since 1999. Over the course of the year nonfarm employment climbed 249,000 a month, or 2.1% for the full year. This was the fastest pace of growth since the end of the 1990s economic boom. The rate of payroll gains in…
